channel.title: Network-Error on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Network-Error on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Docker Network Error — network not found
item.description:
Docker Network Error — network not found

This error occurs when a container tries to connect to a Docker network that doesn’t exist. Networks must be created before containers can use them.

Common Causes

  • Network was not created before running the container
  • Network name typo
  • Network was removed while containers were running
  • Using compose without specifying network explicitly

How to Fix

List Available Networks

docker network ls

Create a New Network

docker network create <network-name>

Connect Container to Network

docker network connect <network-name> <container>

Run Container with Specific Network

docker run --network <network-name> <image>

Inspect Network

docker network inspect <network-name>

Create Bridge Network

docker network create --driver bridge my-network

Examples

# Example 1: Container can't reach another container
docker run --network my-net my-app
# Error: network my-net not found
# Fix: docker network create my-net

# Example 2: Docker Compose network issue
docker compose up
# Error: network myproject_default not found
# Fix: docker compose up --build

# Example 3: Inspect existing networks
docker network ls
# NETWORK ID NAME DRIVER
# abc123 bridge bridge
# def456 host host

Related Errors

  • Port Conflict — port is already allocated
  • Compose Error — docker compose configuration error

item.title: [Solution] JavaScript TypeError: Failed to fetch — Network Error Fix
item.description:
TypeError: Failed to fetch

A TypeError: Failed to fetch is thrown when the fetch() API cannot complete a network request. The error is intentionally vague for security reasons — it doesn’t reveal whether the failure was due to network issues, CORS, DNS resolution, or server errors. The actual cause must be investigated through other means.

Description

Failed to fetch is a blanket error that covers many network-level failures. Browsers intentionally hide the real reason to prevent information leakage. Common underlying causes:

item.title: [Solution] Error 0x800705b4 Windows 11/10 — Operation Timed Out Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Error 0x800705b4 Windows 11/10 — Operation Timed Out Fix

Error 0x800705b4 is an Operation Timed Out error that occurs when a Windows process fails to complete within its allotted time limit. The system abandons the operation and reports the timeout rather than continuing indefinitely.

This error affects both Windows 10 and 11 and commonly appears during Windows Update, software installation, device driver operations, and network communications.

Description

The full error message typically reads:
